meta.title: Georgia Democratic Primary: Voter Flows
meta.subtitle: How modeled favorability maps to the gubernatorial test ballot
meta.methodLabel: Calibrated bridge flows from modeled marginals

feedback_callout.heading: Share Your Feedback
feedback_callout.body: Have questions about these voter-flow bridges or ideas for additional diagnostics? We would love to hear from you.

key_takeaways: This report links modeled favorability buckets to modeled Georgia Democratic gubernatorial test-ballot destinations for each available candidate bridge artifact.
Each candidate section has two flow cards: one for positive sentiment (Very/Somewhat Favorable) and one for negative sentiment (Very/Somewhat Unfavorable).
Use the direction toggle to switch between forward interpretation (favorability to ballot) and reverse interpretation (ballot to favorability composition).
These are calibrated bridge flows constrained to match modeled marginals; they are not raw survey cross-tabs.
methodology_note: Bridge flows are estimated by calibrating a joint distribution between modeled favorability and modeled gubernatorial test-ballot outcomes from the same survey wave. We start from a survey-based weighted seed table, apply light smoothing where needed, and then use iterative proportional fitting (IPF) so the recovered totals match the modeled marginals exactly. This lets us show both how large each flow is overall and where each favorability group currently goes on the ballot. These bridge flows are calibrated synthetic estimates that reconcile modeled marginals, not just raw respondent-level cross-tabs.

direction_modes.label: Favorability → Test Ballot
direction_modes.interpretation: Among people in this favorability bucket, where do they currently go on the ballot?

direction_modes.label: Test Ballot → Favorability
direction_modes.interpretation: Among people in this ballot bucket, what is their favorability composition toward this candidate?
